questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
The equilibrium constant for the reaction
\(\mathrm{SO}_3(\mathrm{g}) \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{SO}_2(\mathrm{~g})+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{O}_2(\mathrm{~g})\)
is \(\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{c}}=4.9 \times 10^{-2}\). The value of \(\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{c}}\) for the reaction given below is \(2 \mathrm{SO}_2(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{O}_2(\mathrm{~g}) \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{SO}_3(\mathrm{~g})\) is :
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{C}}^{\prime}=\left(\frac{1}{\mathrm{~K}_{\mathrm{C}}}\right)^2=\left(\frac{1}{4.9 \times 10^{-2}}\right)^2 \\ & \mathrm{~K}_{\mathrm{C}}^{\prime}=416.49\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
At \(-20^{\circ} \mathrm{C}\) and \(1 \mathrm{~atm}\) pressure, a cylinder is filled with equal number of \(\mathrm{H}_2, \mathrm{I}_2\) and \(\mathrm{HI}\) molecules for the reaction
\(\mathrm{H}_2(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{I}_2(\mathrm{~g}) \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{HI}(\mathrm{g})\), the \(\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{p}}\) for the process is \(x \times 10^{-1}\).
\(\mathrm{x}=\) _____
[Given : \(\mathrm{R}=0.082 \mathrm{~L} \mathrm{~atm} \mathrm{~K}^{-1} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}\)]
questions.solution: $\begin{array}{ll}\Delta \mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{g}}=0 & \mathrm{~K}_{\mathrm{p}}=\frac{\left(\mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{HI}}\right)^2}{\mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{H}_2} \mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{I}_2}}\left(\frac{\mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{T}}}{\mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{T}}}\right)^{\Delta \mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{g}}} \\ \mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{HI}}=\mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{H}_2}=\mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{I}_2} & \text { so } \mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{P}}=1 \\ 1=\mathrm{x} \times 10^{-1} & \mathrm{x}=10\end{array}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: For the reaction <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>4</mn></msub><mo>(</mo><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi><mo>)</mo><mo>⇌</mo><mn>2</mn><msub><mi>NO</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>(</mo><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi><mo>)</mo></math>, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">p</mi></msub><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>492</mn><mo> </mo><mi>atm</mi></math> at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>300</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi><mo>.</mo><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">c</mi></msub></math> for the reaction at same temperature is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>_</mi><mi>_</mi><mi>_</mi><mi>_</mi><mi>_</mi><mi>_</mi><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></math>. (Given :<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>082</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">L</mi><mo> </mo><mi>atm</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi>mol</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>)
questions.solution:
The given reaction is,

 N2O4( g)⇌2NO2( g)

Kp=0.492 atm

T=300 K

KP=KC·(RT)Δng

Δng= number of gaseous molecules of products – number of gaseous molecules of reactants.

Δng=1

By putting the values in the above equation,

⇒Kc=KpRT=0.4920.082×300=2×10-2

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
For the given reaction, choose the correct expression of KC from the following:

Fe3+aq+SCN-aq⇌(FeSCN)2+aq
questions.solution:
The equilibrium constant of a chemical reaction (usually denoted by the symbol K) provides insight into the relationship between the products and reactants when a chemical reaction reaches equilibrium. For example, the equilibrium constant of concentration (denoted by Kc) of a chemical reaction at equilibrium can be defined as the ratio of the concentration of products to the concentration of the reactants, each raised to their respective stoichiometric coefficients. It is important to note that there are different types of equilibrium constants that provide relationships between the products and the reactants of equilibrium reactions in terms of different units.

Equilibrium Constant Formula

Keqb=KfKb=CcDdAaBb=Kc

Where Kc indicates the equilibrium constant measured in moles per litre.

KC=Products Reactants 

KC=FeSCN2+Fe3+SCN-

Hence, the answer is option A.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>A</mi><mfenced><mi>g</mi></mfenced></msub><mo>⇌</mo><msub><mi>B</mi><mfenced><mi>g</mi></mfenced></msub><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mi>C</mi><msup><mn>2</mn><mfenced><mi>g</mi></mfenced></msup></mfrac></math>. The correct relationship between <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>K</mi><mi>P</mi></msub><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>α</mi></math> and equilibrium pressure <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi></math> is
questions.solution:
Ag⇌Bg+12Cgat t=0100at eq.1-ααα2

Total moles at equilibrium: =1-α+α+α2=1+α2

PB=α1+α2p       Pc=α21+α2p      PA=1-α1+α2p

Kp=PB×PC12PA=α1+α2pα21+α2p121-α1+α2p

 Kp=α32p12212(1-α)1+α212

Kp=α32p121-α2+α12

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{K}=\frac{2.303}{\mathrm{t}} \log \frac{1}{0.1} \\ & 4.6 \times 10^{-2}=\frac{2.303}{\mathrm{t}} \\ & \mathrm{t}=50 \mathrm{sec} .\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
During Kinetic study of reaction \(2 A+B \rightarrow C+D\), the following results were obtained :
\(\begin{array}{llll} 
& \text { A [M] } & \text { B [M] } & \text { initial rate of formation of D } \\
\text { I } & 0.1 & 0.1 & 6.0 \times 10^{-3} \\
\text { II } & 0.3 & 0.2 & 7.20 \times 10^{-2} \\
\text { III } & 0.3 & 0.4 & 2.88 \times 10^{-1} \\
\text { IV } & 0.4 & 0.1 & 2.40 \times 10^{-2} \\
\end{array}\)
Based on above data, overall order of the reaction is ______
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{r}=\mathrm{K}[\mathrm{A}]^{\mathrm{x}}[\mathrm{B}]^{\mathrm{y}} \\ & (\mathrm{I}) 6 \times 10^{-3}=\mathrm{K}[0.1]^{\mathrm{x}}[0.1]^{\mathrm{y}} \\ & (\mathrm{IV}) 2.4 \times 10^{-2}=\mathrm{K}[0.4]^{\mathrm{x}}[0.1]^{\mathrm{y}} \\ & \quad(\mathrm{IV}) /(\mathrm{I}) \\ & 4=(4)^{\mathrm{x}} \\ & \quad \mathrm{x}=1 \\ & \mathrm{r}=\mathrm{K}[\mathrm{A}]^{\mathrm{x}}[\mathrm{B}]^{\mathrm{y}}\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & \text { (III) } 2.88 \times 10^{-1}=\mathrm{K}[0.3]^{\mathrm{x}}[0.4]^{\mathrm{y}} \\ & \text { (II) } 7.2 \times 10^{-2}=\mathrm{K}[0.3]^{\mathrm{x}}[0.2]^{\mathrm{y}} \\ & \text { (III)/(II) }\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & 4=2^y \\ & y=2\end{aligned}$
Overall order $=x+y=1+2=3$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
Consider the following reaction
\[
\mathrm{A}+\mathrm{B} \rightarrow \mathrm{C}
\]
The time taken for \(\mathrm{A}\) to become \(1 / 4^{\text {th }}\) of its initial concentration is twice the time taken to become \(1 / 2\) of the same. Also, when the change of concentration of \(B\) is plotted against time, the resulting graph gives a straight line with a negative slope and a positive intercept on the concentration axis.
The overall order of the reaction is _______
questions.solution:
For $1^{\text {st }}$ order reaction $75 \% \text { life }=2 \times 50 \% \text { life }$
So order with respect to A will be first order.
So order with respect to B will be zero. Overall order of reaction $=1+0=1$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
NO2 required for a reaction is produced by decomposition of N2O5 in CCl4 as by equation 2N2O5( g)→4NO2( g)+O2( g)
The initial concentration of N2O5 is 3 mol L-1 and it is 2.75 mol L-1 after 30 minutes.
The rate of formation of NO2 is x×10-3 mol L-1 min-1, value of x is ________.
questions.solution:
The given reaction is,

2 N2O5( g)→4NO2( g)+O2( g)

The initial concentration of N2O5 is 3 mol L-1 and it is 2.75 mol L-1 after 30 minutes.

Now, 

-ΔN2O5Δt=-(2.75-3)30-0 mol L-1 min-1=0.2530

12×ΔN2O5Δt=14 -ΔNO2Δt-ΔNO2Δt=0.2530×2=1.66×10-2 M/Min

≃17×10-3 M/Min

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">r</mi><mo>=</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">k</mi><mfenced close="]" open="["><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></mfenced></math> for a reaction, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>50</mn><mo>%</mo></math> of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> is decomposed in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>120</mn></math> minutes. The time taken for <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>90</mn><mo>%</mo></math> decomposition of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> is ______ minutes.
questions.solution:
r=kA

So, order of reaction =1

t1/2=120 min

k=0.693t1/2

For 90% completion of reaction,

⇒k=2.303tlogaa−xa=100,(a-x)=10

By putting the values in above equation,

⇒0.693t1/2=2.303tlog10010

∴ t=399 min

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
The following data were obtained during the first order thermal decomposition of a gas A at constant volume:

Ag→2 Bg+Cg

S. No                 Time/s                 Total pressure/(atm)

 1.                        0                        0.1

 2.                      115                     0.28

The rate constant of the reaction is _______ ×10-2s-1 (nearest integer)
questions.solution:
t=0t=115 sec. Ag0.10.1-x →2Bg2x+Cg2x

0.1+2x = 0.28

2x=0.18

x=0.09

K=1115ln0.10.1-0.09

=0.0200sec-1

=2×10-2sec-1

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: Time required for completion of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>99</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>9</mn><mo>%</mo></math> of first order reaction is ________ times of half life <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">t</mi><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>/</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mo>)</mo></math> of the reaction
questions.solution:
For first order reaction:
kt=logA0At

A0=Initial concentration of A.

At=Concentration of A at time t.

t99.9%t50%=x=log100100-99.9log10050     =log1000.1log 2     = log 1000log 2    =30.3=10

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
The rate of first order reaction is 0.04 mol L-1 s-1 at 10 minutes and 0.03 mol L-1 s-1 at 20 minutes after initiation. Half life of the reaction is ______ minutes. (Given log2=0.3010,log3=0.4771)

Round off your answer to the nearest integer.
questions.solution:
Rate r =kA0.04=kA0100.03=kA20

⇒A010A20=0.040.03=43

From t=2.303klog A10A2010= 2.303klog 4310= 2.303k(0.6020-0.4771)k= 2.30310×0.1249=2.876×10-2 min-1

t1/2=0.693k=0.6932.876×10-2=0.24×102 min=24 min.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: Integrated rate law equation for a first order gas phase reaction is given by (where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>P</mi><mi>i</mi></msub></math> is initial pressure and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>P</mi><mi>t</mi></msub></math> is total pressure at time <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>t</mi></math>)
questions.solution:
A→           B       +       C

Pi                 0                  0

Pi-x            x                  x

The formula used here is:

k=2.303tlogPiPt

Pt=Pi+x

Pi-x=Pi-Pt+Pi

=2Pi-Pt

k=2.303t×logPi2Pi-Pt

Hence, the answer is option A.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
Consider the following data for the given reaction
2HI(g)→H2( g)+I2( g)

HImolL-1                     0.005         0.01         0.02

Rate molL-1 s-1      7.5×10-4      3.0×10-3       1.2×10-2

The order of the reaction is __________.
questions.solution:
The initial concentrations of HI as 0.005, 0.01, and 0.02 mol/L, respectively, and the corresponding initial rates as given:

HImolL-1                     0.005         0.01         0.02

Rate molL-1 s-1      7.5×10-4      3.0×10-3       1.2×10-2

 For the reaction, rate law is
rate=kHIP
P is the order of the reaction.
From the given data
7.5×10-4=k0.05P-------(1)
3×10-3=k0.01P  --------(2)
1.2×10-2=k0.02P ---------(3)
On dividing Equation (3) by equation (2)
12×10-33×10-3=k0.02Pk0.01P
⇒2P=22
⇒P=2

questions.solution: In the cathode reaction manganese $(\mathrm{Mn})$ is reduced from the +4 oxidation state to the +3 state.

questions.q: The quantity of silver deposited when one coulomb charge is passed through \(\mathrm{AgNO}_3\) solution :
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{W}=\mathrm{ZIt} \\ & \mathrm{W}=\mathrm{ZQ} \\ & \mathrm{Q}=\frac{\mathrm{W}}{\mathrm{Z}} \\ & \mathrm{W}=\mathrm{ZQ}=\text { (electrochemical equivalent) }\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.solution:
Anodic half cell
$\underline{\text { Gas - gas ion electrode }}$
$\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{H}_{2(\mathrm{~g})} \rightarrow \mathrm{H}_{(\mathrm{aq})}^{+}+\mathrm{e}^{-}$
Cathodic Reaction
Metal-metal insoluble salt anion electrode
$\mathrm{Ag}_{(\mathrm{aq})}^{+}+\mathrm{e}^{-} \rightarrow \mathrm{Ag}_{(\mathrm{s})}$
$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{AgCl}_{(\mathrm{s})} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{Ag}_{(\mathrm{aq})}^{+}+\mathrm{Cl}_{(\mathrm{aq})}^{-} \\ & \mathrm{AgCl}_{(\mathrm{s})}+\mathrm{e}^{-} \rightarrow \mathrm{Ag}_{(\mathrm{s})}+\mathrm{Cl}_{(\mathrm{aq})}^{-}\end{aligned}$
Overall redox reaction
$\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{H}_{2(\mathrm{~g})}+\mathrm{AgCl}_{(\mathrm{s})} \rightarrow \mathrm{H}_{(\mathrm{aq})}^{+}+\mathrm{Cl}_{(\mathrm{aq})}^{-}+\mathrm{Ag}_{(\mathrm{s})}$
Cell Representation
Pt $\left|\mathrm{H}_{2(\mathrm{~g})}\right| \mathrm{kCl}_{\text {(sol) }}\left|\mathrm{AgCl}_{\text {(s) }}\right| \mathrm{Ag}$

questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.solution: A-IV, B-III, C-I, D-II

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: One Faraday of electricity liberates <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>–</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math> gram atom of copper from copper sulphate, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi></math> is______.
questions.solution:
One Faraday of electricity means it is equivalent to one mole of electrons charge.

Cu2++2e–→Cu

2 Faraday →1 mol Cu

1 Faraday →0.5 mol Cu deposit

0.5 mol=0.5 g atom=5×10–1

x=5

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
Consider the following redox reaction: MnO4-+H++H2C2O4⇌Mn2++H2O+CO2

The standard reduction potentials are given as below Ered°

E0MnO4-/Mn2+=+1.51 V;  E0CO2/H2C2O4=-0.49 V

If the equilibrium constant of the above reaction is given as Keq=10x, then the value of x= _______ (nearest integer)
questions.solution:
For the reaction at equilibrium,

2MnO4-+6H++5H2C2O4⇌2Mn2++8H2O+10CO2.

Given : EMnO4-/ Mn2+0=1.51 V and E0H2C2O4/CO2=0.49 V.

So,  Ecell0 =1.51 + 0.49=2 V.

Number of electrons involved in reaction can be calculated as follows:

Mn+7  changes Mn2+ so, each  Mn gain five electrons.

n = 10

Now, Ecell0 =0.0591nlog K

log K =10×20.0591

log K =338.4

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: The Molarity \((\mathrm{M})\) of an aqueous solution containing \(5.85 \mathrm{~g}\) of NaCl in \(500 \mathrm{~mL}\) water is : (Given : Molar Mass \(\mathrm{Na}: 23\) and \(\mathrm{Cl}: 35.5 \mathrm{gmol}^{-1}\) )
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{M}=\frac{\mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{NaCl}}}{\left.\mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{sol}} \text { (in } \mathrm{L}\right)} \\ & \mathrm{M}=\frac{\frac{5.85}{58.5}}{0.5}=0.2 \mathrm{M}\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
The potential for the given half cell at 298K is -............ ×10-2V.

2H(aq)++2e-→H2( g)

H+=1M,PH2=2 atm

(Given2.303 RT/F=0.06 V, log2=0.3)
questions.solution:
The Nernst equation for the given cell reaction 2H++2e-→H2 is 

E=E°-0.062×log pH2H+2

E=0-0.062log212

E=-0.03×0.3=-0.009=-9×10-3

E=-0.9×10-2V

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
\(2.7 \mathrm{~kg}\) of each of water and acetic acid are mixed. The freezing point of the solution will be \(-x^{\circ} \mathrm{C}\). Consider the acetic acid does not dimerise in water, nor dissociates in water. \(x=\) ______ (nearest integer)
[Given: Molar mass of water \(=18 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}\), acetic acid \(=60 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}\)
\(\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{f}} \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}: 1.86 \mathrm{~K} \mathrm{~kg} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}\)
\(\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{f}}\) acetic acid: \(3.90 \mathrm{~K} \mathrm{~kg} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}\)
freezing point: \(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}=273 \mathrm{~K}\), acetic acid \(=290 \mathrm{~K}\)]
questions.solution:
As moles of water $>$ moles of $\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{COOH}$ water is solvent.
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{T}_{\mathrm{F}}^{\circ}-\left(\mathrm{T}_{\mathrm{F}}\right)_{\mathrm{S}}=\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{F}} \times \mathrm{M} \\
& 0-\left(\mathrm{T}_{\mathrm{F}}\right)_{\mathrm{S}}=1.86 \times \frac{2700 / 60}{2700 / 1000} \\
& \left(\mathrm{~T}_{\mathrm{F}}\right)_{\mathrm{S}}=-31^{\circ} \mathrm{C} .
\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: The vapour pressure of pure benzene and methyl benzene at \(27^{\circ} \mathrm{C}\) is given as 80 Torr and 24 Torr, respectively. The mole fraction of methyl benzene in vapour phase, in equilibrium with an equimolar mixture of those two liquids (ideal solution) at the same temperature is _______ \(\times 10^{-2}\) (nearest integer)
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} \mathrm{X}_{\text {methylbenzene }} & =0.5 \\ \mathrm{Y}_{\text {methylbenzene }} & =\frac{\mathrm{P}_{\text {methylbenzene }}}{\mathrm{P}_{\text {total }}} \\ \mathrm{Y}_{\text {methylbenzene }} & =\frac{0.5 \times 24}{0.5 \times 80+0.5 \times 24} \\ & =\frac{12}{40+12}=0.23=23 \times 10^{-2}\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: What happens to freezing point of benzene when small quantity of napthalene is added to benzene?
questions.solution:
Depression in Freezing point can be calculated as,

∆Tf=i×Kf×m

When a non-volatile component is added to the solvent, its freezing point decreases. Here, Napthalene is a non-volatile substance which decreases the freezing point of benzene.

On addition of naphthalene to benzene there is depression in freezing point of benzene.

Hence, the answer is option D.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: Identify the mixture that shows positive deviations from Raoult's Law
questions.solution:
The pressure exerted by the vapours above the liquid surface in equllibrium with the liquid at a given temperature is called vapour pressure.  
    
    When a non-volatile solute is dissolved in a solvent, its vapour pressure decreases. This decrease in vapour pressure is known as “Lowering of vapour pressure”. The ratio of the  Lowering of vapour pressure to the vapour pressure of the pure solvent is called Relative lowering of vapour pressure. It is a Colligative Property.
    Statement:  Raoult's law  states that “at a given temperature, for a solution of volatile liquids the partial vapour pressure  of each component in solution is equal to the product of the vapour pressure of the pure component and its mole fraction”.Psolution ≠ P0solvent × Xsolvent
    For an ideal solution, PS = PA + PB  = PA0 × XA  +  PB0 × XB
    
    Definition: An ideal solution is one which obeys  Raoult's law  over the entire range of concentration.

 A non-ideal solution does not obey Raoult's law  as Psolution ≠ P0solvent × Xsolvent  The vapour pressure of such a solution is either higher or lower than the product. When it is higher, the solution exhibits a positive deviation. When it is  lower, it exhibits a negative deviation from Raoult’s law.

 A positive deviation from Raoult’s Law arises when the forces between the particles in the mixture
 are weaker than the mean of the forces between the particles in the pure solvent. There is some
hydrogen bonding in pure CH32CO. However, when CS2 is added to  CH32CO, the molecules of CS2 tend to occupy the space  between CH32CO  molecules. Consequently some hydrogen bonds in CH32CO molecules break and the attractive forces in CH32CO molecules get weakened.
The escaping tendency of CH32CO  and CS2   molecules from the solution will increase.  Because of this, effective
 number of particles will decrease in the solution. Hence vapour pressure of the solution will be
 greater than the vapour pressure as expected from Raoult’s law.  So this will show positive deviation from Raoult’s law.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: The osmotic pressure of a dilute solution is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>7</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mn>5</mn></msup><mo> </mo><mi>Pa</mi></math> at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>273</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi></math>. Osmotic pressure of the same solution at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>283</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>_</mi><mi>_</mi><mi>_</mi><mi>_</mi><mi>_</mi><mi>_</mi><mi>_</mi><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mn>4</mn></msup><msup><mi>Nm</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></math>.(Nearest integer)
questions.solution:
Osmotic pressure can be calculated as,

π=CRT

π is the osmotic pressure

C is the molar concentration of the solute in the solution

R is the universal gas constant

Here given,

 T1=273KT2=283K 

π1=CRT1By putting the values in this equation, we get,7×105=C×R×273CR=7×105273π2=CRT2     =  7×105273×283     =72.56×104Nm−2

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: The density of ' \(x\) ' \(\mathrm{M}\) solution (' \(X\) ' molar) of \(\mathrm{NaOH}\) is \(1.12 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~mL}^{-1}\), while in molality, the concentration of the solution is \(3 \mathrm{~m}(3 \mathrm{molal})\). Then \(x\) is (Given : Molar mass of \(\mathrm{NaOH}\) is \(40 \mathrm{~g} / \mathrm{mol}\) )
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \text { Molality }=\frac{1000 \times \mathrm{M}}{1000 \times \mathrm{d}-\mathrm{M} \times(\mathrm{Mw})_{\text {solute }}} \\ & 3=\frac{1000 \times \mathrm{x}}{1000 \times 1.12-(\mathrm{x} \times 40)} \\ & \mathrm{x}=3\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: Molarity \((\mathrm{M})\) of an aqueous solution containing \(x \mathrm{~g}\) of anhyd. \(\mathrm{CuSO}_4\) in \(500 \mathrm{~mL}\) solution at \(32{ }^{\circ} \mathrm{C}\) is \(2 \times 10^{-1} \mathrm{M}\). Its molality will be ______ \(\times 10^{-3} \mathrm{~m}\). (nearest integer). [Given density of the solution \(=1.25 \mathrm{~g} / \mathrm{mL}\)]
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \begin{aligned} & \mathrm{M}_{\text {sol }^{\mathrm{I}}}=\mathrm{v}_{\text {sol }^{\mathrm{n}}} \times \mathrm{d}_{\text {sol }^{\mathrm{n}}} \\ &=500 \times 1.25=625 \mathrm{~g} \\ & \text { Mass of solute }(\mathrm{x})=0.2 \times 0.5 \times 159.5 \\ & \quad=15.95\end{aligned} \\ & \mathrm{n}_{\text {solute }}=0.1, \quad \\ & \text { Mass of solvent = Mass of solution }- \text { Mass of solute } \\ & =625-15.95 \\ & =609.05 \\ & \mathrm{~m}=\frac{0.1}{\frac{609.05}{1000}} \\ & \mathrm{~m}=0.164=164 \times 10^{-3}\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: The molarity of 1L orthophosphoric acid H3PO4 having 70% purity by weight (specific gravity 1.54g cm−3) is ______ M. (Molar mass of H3PO4=98 g mol-1)
questions.solution:
Specific gravity (density) =1.54g/cc.

Volume =1L=1000 ml

Mass of solution =1.54×1000

=1540g

% purity of H3PO4 is 70%

So weight of H3PO4=0.7×1540=1078g

Mole of H3PO4=massmolar mass=107898=11

Molarity= No. of moles of soluteVolume of solution in L

Molarity =111L=11M

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: Choose the Incorrect Statement about Dalton's Atomic Theory
options: chemical reactions involve reorganization of atoms
Compounds are formed when atoms of different elements combine in any ratio.

Compounds are formed when atoms of different elements combine in any ratio. All the atoms of a given element have identical properties including identical 
mass.
questions.solution: In compound atoms of different elements combine in fixed ratio by mass.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
The electronic configuration of Einsteinium is :
(Given atomic number of Einsteinium \(=99)\)
questions.solution: Einsteinium (atomic No $=99):[R n] 5 \mathrm{f}^{11} 6 \mathrm{~d}^0 7 \mathrm{~s}^2$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
Match List I with List II
\(\begin{array}{|l|l|l|l|}
\hline & \text{ List - I (Element) } & & \text{List - II (Electronic configuration)} \\
\hline \text { A. } & \mathrm{N} & \text { I. } & {[\mathrm{Ar}] 3 \mathrm{~d}^{10} 4 \mathrm{~s}^2 4 \mathrm{p}^5 \mathrm{AR}} \\
\hline \text { B. } & \mathrm{S} & \text { II. } & {[\mathrm{Ne}] 3 \mathrm{~s}^2 3 \mathrm{p}^4} \\
\hline \text { C. } & \mathrm{Br} & \text { III. } & {[\mathrm{He}] 2 \mathrm{~s}^2 2 \mathrm{p}^3} \\
\hline \text { D. } & \mathrm{Kr} & \text { IV. } & {[\mathrm{Ar}] 3 \mathrm{~d}^{10} 4 \mathrm{~s}^2 4 \mathrm{p}^6} \\
\hline
\end{array}\)
Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
questions.solution:
(A) ${ }_7 \mathrm{~N}:[\mathrm{He}] 2 \mathrm{~s}^2 2 \mathrm{p}^3$
(B) ${ }_{16} \mathrm{~S}:[\mathrm{Ne}] 2 \mathrm{~s}^2 3 \mathrm{p}^4$
(C) ${ }_{35} \mathrm{Br}:[\mathrm{Ar}] 3 \mathrm{~d}^{10} 4 \mathrm{~s}^2 4 \mathrm{p}^5$
(D) ${ }_{36} \mathrm{Kr}:[\mathrm{Ar}] 3 \mathrm{~d}^{10} 4 \mathrm{~s}^2 4 \mathrm{p}^6$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mL</mi></math> of gaseous hydrocarbon on combustion gives <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>40</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mL</mi></math> of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>CO</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mfenced><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></mfenced></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>50</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mL</mi></math> of water vapour. Total number of carbon and hydrogen atoms in the hydrocarbon is _______.
questions.solution:
The combustion reaction can be represented as 

CxHy+x+y4O2→xCO2+yH2O

At STP, 1 mole of any gas occupies 22.4 L

10 mL of hydrocarbon, 50 mL of water and 40 mL of carbon dioxide corresponds to 0.45, 2.23,  and 1.79 moles respectively.

Hence, the combustion of 1 mole of hydrocarbon will produce 5 moles of water and 4 moles of carbon dioxide.

Thus, the molecular formula contains 4 carbon atoms and 10 hydrogen atoms.

The hydrocarbon is C4H10 and the combustion reaction is 

C4H10+6.5O2→4CO2+5H2O

x+y=4+10=14

Hence the Total number of carbon and hydrogen atoms in the hydrocarbon is 14.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: The four quantum numbers for the electron in the outer most orbital of potassium (atomic no. <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>19</mn></math>) are
questions.solution:
The set of numbers used to describe the position and energy of the electron in an atom are called quantum numbers. There are four quantum numbers, namely, principal, azimuthal, magnetic and spin quantum numbers.

Electronic configuration of K is:

K= Atomic number=191s22s22p63s23p64s1

The outermost electron is in 4s1

∴ n=4, l=0, m=0, s=+12

Hence, the answer is option B.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \lambda=\frac{\mathrm{h}}{\mathrm{mv}} \\ & \lambda=\frac{\mathrm{hv}}{\mathrm{mv}^2} \\ & \frac{\mathrm{mv}^2}{\mathrm{~h}}=\frac{\mathrm{v}}{\lambda}=\mathrm{v} \text { (frequency) } \\ & \text { Given } \frac{1}{2} \mathrm{mv}^2=2.18 \times 10^{-18} \mathrm{~J} \\ & \mathrm{~h}=6.6 \times 10^{-34} \\ & \mathrm{v}=\frac{4.36 \times 10^{-18}}{6.6 \times 10^{-34}}=660.60 \times 10^{13} \mathrm{~Hz} \\ & \approx 661 \times 10^{13} \mathrm{~Hz}\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: The correct set of four quantum numbers for the valence electron of rubidium atom <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi>Z</mi><mo> </mo><mo>=</mo><mo> </mo><mn>37</mn><mo>)</mo></math> is:
questions.solution:
Electronic configuration of Rb=Kr5s1

Rb is alkali metal with valence electron in 5s subshell. Hence, n=5

Rb=[Kr]5 s1

n=5

l=0

m=0

s=+12 or -12

Hence, the answer is option A,

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: The number of radial node/s for <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">p</mi></math> orbital is:
questions.solution:
A point or plane of an orbital where the electron density of the electron of an orbital is zero is called a node. Nodes can be two types; one is a radial node and another one is an angular node.

Number of radial node=n-l-1

Now, for 3p orbital, the value of n is 3, and the azimuthal quantum number is 1. Therefore, the number of radial node is, n-l-1=(3-1-1)=1

Hence, ans will be A

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: The enthalpy of formation of ethane \(\left(\mathrm{C}_2 \mathrm{H}_6\right)\) from ethylene by addition of hydrogen where the bond-energies of \(\mathrm{C}-\mathrm{H}, \mathrm{C}-\mathrm{C}, \mathrm{C}=\mathrm{C}, \mathrm{H}-\mathrm{H}\) are \(414 \mathrm{~kJ}, 347 \mathrm{~kJ}, 615 \mathrm{~kJ}\) and \(435 \mathrm{~kJ}\) respectively is \(\qquad\) \(\mathrm{kJ}\)
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{C}_2 \mathrm{H}_4(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{H}_2(\mathrm{~g}) \rightarrow \mathrm{C}_2 \mathrm{H}_6(\mathrm{~g}) \\ & \Delta \mathrm{H}=\mathrm{BE}(\mathrm{C}=\mathrm{C})+4 \mathrm{BE}(\mathrm{C}-\mathrm{H})+\mathrm{BE}(\mathrm{H}-\mathrm{H}) \\ & -\mathrm{BE}(\mathrm{C}-\mathrm{C})-6 \mathrm{BE}(\mathrm{C}-\mathrm{H}) \\ & \Delta \mathrm{H}=\mathrm{BE}(\mathrm{C}=\mathrm{C})+\mathrm{BE}(\mathrm{H}-\mathrm{H})-\mathrm{BE}(\mathrm{C}-\mathrm{C}) \\ & -2 \mathrm{BE}(\mathrm{C}-\mathrm{H}) \\ & =615+435-347-2 \times 414 \\ & =-125 \mathrm{~kJ}\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
Combustion of 1 mole of benzene is expressed at
\(\mathrm{C}_6 \mathrm{H}_6(\mathrm{l})+\frac{15}{2} \mathrm{O}_2(\mathrm{~g}) \rightarrow 6 \mathrm{CO}_2(\mathrm{~g})+3 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l}) \text {. }\)
The standard enthalpy of combustion of \(2 \mathrm{~mol}\) of benzene is \(-^{\prime} x^{\prime} \mathrm{kJ}\).
\(x=\) ______
Given:
1. standard Enthalpy of formation of \(1 \mathrm{~mol}\) of \(\mathrm{C}_6 \mathrm{H}_6(\mathrm{l})\), for the reaction \(6 \mathrm{C}\) (graphite) \(+3 \mathrm{H}_2(\mathrm{~g}) \rightarrow \mathrm{C}_6 \mathrm{H}_6(\mathrm{l})\) is \(48.5 \mathrm{~kJ} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}\).
2. Standard Enthalpy of formation of \(1 \mathrm{~mol}\) of \(\mathrm{CO}_2(\mathrm{~g})\), for the reaction \(\mathrm{C}\) (graphite) \(+\mathrm{O}_2(\mathrm{~g}) \rightarrow \mathrm{CO}_2(\mathrm{~g})\) is \(-393.5 \mathrm{~kJ} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}\).
3. Standard and Enthalpy of formation of \(1 \mathrm{~mol}\) of \(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})\), for the reaction \(\mathrm{H}_2(\mathrm{~g})+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{O}_2(\mathrm{~g}) \rightarrow \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})\) is \(-286 \mathrm{~kJ} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}\).
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & 6 \mathrm{C}(\text { graphite })+3 \mathrm{H}_2(\mathrm{~g}) \rightarrow \mathrm{C}_6 \mathrm{H}_6(\ell) ; \Delta \mathrm{H}=48.5 \mathrm{~kJ} / \mathrm{mol} \\ & \mathrm{C}(\text { graphite })+\mathrm{O}_2(\mathrm{~g}) \rightarrow \mathrm{CO}_2(\mathrm{~g}) ; \Delta \mathrm{H}=-393.5 \mathrm{~kJ} / \mathrm{mol} \\ & \mathrm{H}_2^{(\mathrm{g})}+\frac{1}{2}(\mathrm{~g}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}(\ell) ; \Delta \mathrm{H}=-286 \mathrm{~kJ} / \mathrm{mol} \\ & \text { equation }-(1) \times 1+(2) \times 6+(3) \times 3 \\ & -48.5-6 \times 393.5-3 \times 286 \\ & =-3267.5 \mathrm{~kJ} \text { for } 1 \mathrm{~mol} \\ & =-6535 \mathrm{~kJ} \text { for } 2 \mathrm{~mol} \\ & \text { Ans. } 6535 \mathrm{~kJ}\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
An ideal gas, \(\overline{\mathrm{C}}_{\mathrm{v}}=\frac{5}{2} \mathrm{R}\), is expanded adiabatically against a constant pressure of 1 atm untill it doubles in volume. If the initial temperature and pressure is \(298 \mathrm{~K}\) and \(5 \mathrm{~atm}\), respectively then the final temperature is _______ \(\mathrm{K}\) (nearest integer).
[\(\overline{\mathrm{C}}_{\mathrm{v}}\) is the molar heat capacity at constant volume]
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \Delta \mathrm{U}=\mathrm{q}+\mathrm{w}(\mathrm{q}=0) \\ & \mathrm{nC}_{\mathrm{V}} \Delta \mathrm{T}=-\mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{ext}}\left(\mathrm{V}_2-\mathrm{V}_1\right) \\ & \mathrm{V}_2=2 \mathrm{~V}_1 \\ & \frac{\mathrm{nRT}_2}{\mathrm{P}_2}=\frac{2 \mathrm{nRT}_1}{\mathrm{P}_1} \\ & \mathrm{P}_1=5, \mathrm{~T}_1=298 \\ & \mathrm{P}_2=\frac{5 \mathrm{~T}_2}{2 \times 298} \\ & \mathrm{n} \frac{5}{2} \mathrm{R}\left(\mathrm{T}_2-\mathrm{T}_1\right)=-1\left(\frac{\mathrm{nRT}_2}{\mathrm{P}_1}-\frac{\mathrm{nRT}_1}{\mathrm{P}_1}\right) \end{aligned}$
Put $\mathrm{T}_1=298$ and $\mathrm{P}_2=\frac{5 \mathrm{~T}_2}{2 \times 298}$ Solve and we get $T_2=274.16 \mathrm{~K}$ $\mathrm{T}_2 \approx 274 \mathrm{~K}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: If three moles of an ideal gas at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>300</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi></math> expand isothermally from <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>30</mn><msup><mi>dm</mi><mn>3</mn></msup></math> to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>45</mn><msup><mi>dm</mi><mn>3</mn></msup></math> against a constant opposing pressure of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>80</mn><mi>kPa</mi></math>, then the amount of heat transferred is __________J.
questions.solution:
Given,

 V1=30 dm3V2=45 dm3P=80 kPa

Using, first law of thermodynamics,
ΔU=Q+W

Q is the heat given or lost

ΔU is the change in internal energy

W is the work done

ΔU=0 : Process is isothermal

Q=-W

W=-Pext ΔV: Irreversible

W=-Pext (V2-V1)

=-80×103(45-30)×10-3

=-1200 J

Hence, the amount of heat transferred is 1200 J

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
If 5 moles of an ideal gas expands from 10L to a volume of 100L at 300K under isothermal and reversible condition then work, w, is −x J. The value of x is -_______.

(Given R=8.314 J K-1 mol-1)
questions.solution:
Given,

 T=300K,V1=10L,V2=100L,n=5

It is isothermal reversible expansion, so work done negative

W=−2.303nRT logV2V1

=−2.303×5×8.314×300 log10010

=−28720.713J

≡−28721J
